channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft’s claim that Arm-based Copilot+ PCs are “fastest, most intelligent Windows PCs” is debunked on X – and the community’s verdict isn’t wrong
item.description: X fact-checkers were quickly on the case when Microsoft made its claims, and the ensuing takedown was pretty brutal.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: China bans exports to US of gallium, germanium, antimony in response to chip sanctions
item.description: China has announced a ban on exports to the United States of gallium, germanium and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How Amazon influencer Sydney Nicole Gifford’s lawsuit could change the industry
item.description: In a first-of-its-kind lawsuit, one TikTok content creator is suing another for copying her “neutral, beige, and cream aesthetic.” After a judge ruled the case should go forward last month, the matter now goes to U.S. District Court for the Western District of Texas for final disposition.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: What is 'lake-effect snow'? Warm air from large bodies of water is the key ingredient
item.description: The lake-effect snow that has fallen in parts of upstate New York, Pennsylvania and Michigan is the result of cold, moist air that blew over the Great Lakes region.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FTC opens Microsoft antitrust investigation that Trump administration must carry on or drop
item.description: Antitrust enforcers with the Federal Trade Commission have opened a wide-ranging investigation into Microsoft's business practices, starting a big legal project that an incoming Trump administration must take up or abandon.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: MRDIMM: Why your next server will have a new kind of memory
item.description: Intel is rolling out a new form of memory that requires no changes to existing hardware or software, is usable by the competition, and promises up to a 40% increase in memory performance for certain workloads. The product is called Multiplexed Rank Dual Inline Memory Module, or MRDIMM.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Robots perform like human surgeons by just watching videos
item.description: University scientists have trained a robotic surgical system to do work with the skill of human doctors, possibly transforming the future of surgeries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amazon worker strike: 5 things to know
item.description: Thousands of Amazon workers around the world went on strike Friday, demanding more pay and better working conditions during one of the retail giant's busiest weekends of the year.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online advertising
item.description: The Competition Bureau of Canada has filed an antitrust lawsuit against Google, accusing the tech giant of abusing its dominant position in the online advertising technology market to stifle competition and harm advertisers, publishers, and consumers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online ads
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's antitrust watchdog said Thursday it is suing Google over alleged anticompetitive conduct in the tech giant’s online advertising business and wants the company to sell off two of its ad tech services and pay a penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Intel’s $7.9B subsidy deal comes at a high price for the chipmaker
item.description: Intel’s $7.86 billion subsidy agreement with the US government imposes strict conditions on the company’s ability to divest stakes in its chipmaking division if the unit becomes independent.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Kai Trump shows Elon Musk awkwardly tutoring Trump in rocket science
item.description: While Trump's aspiring influencer granddaughter happily shared video of her joining him at Musk's latest SpaceX launch, she also revealed moments of the president-elect looking 'out of depth' and 'intimidated.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Amazon's $4B bet on an AI startup
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australia's social media ban for kids is closer to becoming law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia’s House of Representatives on Wednesday passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years old from social media, leaving it to the Senate to finalize the world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pentagon drafts large-scale government AI contract
item.description: Potential bidders have a little under a month to offer feedback on the draft RFP for what DoD is calling the Advancing AI Multiple Award Contract. The post Pentagon drafts large-scale government AI contract first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Linux command line for beginners: 25 essential commands
item.description: Getting started on the Linux command line might seem overwhelming at first, but the many commands you need to use will fall into place more quickly than you might imagine. Let’s start with some introductory but very important commands.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ask the CIO: Defense Commissary Agency
item.description: Learn how agencies are advancing their cybersecurity posture in today’s complex threat landscape. The post Ask the CIO: Defense Commissary Agency first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network
